source.name: CDTFA Open Data Portal — Taxable Sales & Summary of Revenues
source.publisher: California Department of Tax and Fee Administration

cadence: quarterly, ~5-month lag on taxable sales; Summary of Revenues annual
caveats: Taxable sales are the tax base, not tax revenue: multiply by the applicable rate to approximate tax, but only the fund split from CDTFA's Summary of Revenues shows actual dollars received, and its latest complete fiscal year trails the budget bar.
Individual seller-permit data is confidential under R&TC §7056; city cells below CDTFA's disclosure threshold arrive suppressed and are counted here, never estimated.
Business-type and city tables cannot be crossed — CDTFA publishes type-by-state and city-by-total only, so 'which business types in which city' is not answerable from public data.

budget_reference.note: The budget waterfall shows the Department of Finance's enacted General Fund estimate for 2025-26. CDTFA's Summary of Revenues shows the General Fund actually received $35.0B in 2024-25, the latest complete fiscal year — close measures, different years, shown side by side.

business_types.label: Building Material and Garden Equipment and Supplies
Real Estate and Rental and Leasing
Sporting Goods, Hobby, Musical Instruments, Book Stores
Administrative and Support and Waste Management and Remediation Services
Mining, Quarrying, and Oil and Gas Extraction

data.base_note: Business-type and city figures are TAXABLE SALES — the base the tax is applied to, not tax collected. The General Fund receives its slice of the statewide rate; the fund split above shows who actually gets the money.
